text.title: TDD with Red and Green in Separate Agents Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: One agent runs both `RED` and `GREEN` prompts in sequence using the same tool set. Edits the test to pass when stuck.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped sub-agent infra; single agent reused.
antipattern.fix: Wire two actual sub-agents with disjoint tools; assert in the orchestrator.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator passes the acceptance criterion to GREEN 'as context'. GREEN paraphrases the spec into the impl, test passes for wrong reasons.
antipattern.fix: Strip the AC from GREEN's prompt; pass only the failing test diff + runner output.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator records 'TDD complete' without proving RED happened. CI logs lack a red run.
antipattern.root-cause: Merge gate not tied to a recorded transition.
antipattern.fix: Require an artifact (CI log line, structured event) proving the RED state was observed before GREEN.
